aspects.high_concept: Fae-Lands Warrior Stranded in the Void
Resourceful Healer Who Keeps Everyone Together
aspects.trouble: Can't Say No to Someone in Need

rooms.name: Central Clearing
rooms.description: The heart of the skerry — a small patch of solid ground amid the void. A faint warmth radiates from the world seed's presence below. From here, paths lead to the other areas of your fragile home.

rooms.name: Basic Shelter
rooms.description: A rough structure cobbled together from salvaged hull plating and fabric. It keeps the void-wind out, barely. There's room for a few people to sleep, though comfort is a generous word for it.

rooms.name: The Hollow
rooms.description: A natural depression in the skerry's surface where the world seed has taken root. Faint green light pulses from below, and you can feel its awareness pressing gently against your mind. This is where you commune with the seed and feed it motes.

rooms.name: Landing Pad
rooms.description: The edge of the skerry where the void begins. From here, you can see the distant glimmer of the debris field to the north, the soft glow of the coral thicket to the east, and the cold glitter of the frozen wreck to the west. This is where explorers depart and return.

expandable_rooms.name: Workshop
expandable_rooms.description: A proper workspace with a sturdy bench and basic tools mounted on the walls. Here, salvage becomes something useful.

expandable_rooms.name: Garden
expandable_rooms.description: A patch of cultivated ground where seeds from the coral thicket have taken hold. Small green shoots push up through the soil, nourished by the world seed's ambient energy.

expandable_rooms.name: Water Collection
expandable_rooms.description: Crystal shards arranged in a precise pattern collect and purify moisture from the void-mist. The water tastes faintly of minerals but is clean and safe.

expandable_rooms.name: Lookout Post
expandable_rooms.description: A raised platform built from ancient alloys, offering a clear view in all directions. From here, you can spot approaching threats — or opportunities — long before they arrive.

aspects.high_concept: Mysterious Wanderer Who Found the Skerry First
aspects.trouble: Secrets That Could Help or Harm

dialogue.greeting: Varis nods as you approach. 'The void is restless today. Can you feel it?'
dialogue.idle: 'There's work to be done. The skerry won't grow itself — well, Tuft will, but it needs help.'
dialogue.happy: 'This place is starting to feel like it could be something. Keep at it.'
dialogue.quest: 'The frozen wreck to the west... I've seen lights there that shouldn't exist. Be careful if you go.'

aspects.high_concept: Enthusiastic Scavenger With an Eye for Value

dialogue.greeting: A young woman waves from atop a cargo crate. 'Oh! A person! A real, actual person! I haven't seen anyone in ages!'
dialogue.idle: 'So when do we start scavenging? I know all the good spots around here.'
dialogue.happy: 'I love having people around. The void was so quiet before.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You have a place to stay? With actual walls? I'm in. Absolutely in. When do we leave?'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I mean, it sounds nice, but... are you sure it's safe? Maybe come back later?'

aspects.high_concept: Cautious Mechanic Who's Seen Too Much Go Wrong

dialogue.greeting: A stocky figure looks up from the console they're tinkering with. 'Another survivor? Don't touch anything.'
dialogue.idle: 'If you need something fixed, I'm your person. Just don't expect miracles.'
dialogue.happy: 'I have to admit, this shelter is better than a dead control room. Good work.'
dialogue.recruit_success: Dax looks around the ruined control room, then sighs. 'Fine. Can't be worse than this. Lead the way.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I don't know you. Why would I leave the only shelter I know to follow a stranger? Come back when I have a reason to trust you.'

aspects.high_concept: Scared Teenager Trying to Be Brave

dialogue.greeting: A teenager peers out from behind a lean-to, eyes wide. 'Are... are you friendly? Please be friendly.'
dialogue.idle: 'I'm trying to help. Just tell me what to do and I'll do it. Promise.'
dialogue.happy: 'I feel safe here. For the first time in... I don't even know how long.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You want me to come with you? Really? Yes! I mean — yes. Definitely. Let's go.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I want to, but... what if it's not safe? What if we get lost on the way?'

aspects.high_concept: Practical Farmer Who Makes Things Grow

dialogue.greeting: A weathered woman looks up from her coral garden. 'Hm. You look like you could use a meal. Sit down.'
dialogue.idle: 'The garden needs tending. These coral-seeds don't plant themselves.'
dialogue.happy: 'The soil here responds to Tuft. I've never seen anything like it. We could grow real food.'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'You've got a world seed and a place to put down roots? Show me this skerry of yours. If it's real, I'll stay.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'Talk is cheap in the void. Come back when you have something real to show me.'

dialogue.greeting: A thin man adjusts spectacles made from crystal shards. 'Fascinating — another living being. Tell me, can you read Eliok script?'
dialogue.idle: 'The inscriptions on this ship... they tell a story. A world that grew from a single seed. Sound familiar?'
dialogue.happy: 'Access to Tuft changes everything. If I could study the world seed directly...'
dialogue.recruit_success: 'A world seed? An actual living world seed? I need to study it. Take me to it immediately.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'I appreciate the offer, but my research here isn't finished. Perhaps once I've translated the vault inscriptions.'

aspects.high_concept: Battle-Hardened Warrior of the Old Ships

dialogue.greeting: A scarred woman rises from a crouch, hand on a makeshift blade. 'State your business. Quickly.'
dialogue.idle: 'Don't bore me with talk. If there's fighting to be done, I'm ready.'
dialogue.happy: 'This skerry... it's worth defending. I'll make sure nothing threatens it.'
dialogue.recruit_success: Angya sheathes her blade. 'You fight well. That's all I needed to see. Lead on.'
dialogue.recruit_fail: 'You want me to follow you? Prove you're worth following. Fight me.'

debris_field.name: The Debris Field
debris_field.description: A slowly tumbling mass of broken hull plating, cargo containers, and twisted metal. The remains of some great vessel, shattered and scattered across this pocket of void.

rooms.name: Edge of the Debris Field
rooms.description: Jagged metal fragments drift lazily at the boundary of the debris field. A faint hum resonates through the larger pieces, as if the wreckage still remembers being whole. The way back to the skerry is clear to the south.

rooms.name: Shattered Cargo Bay
rooms.description: The remnants of a cargo hold, its walls peeled open like a tin can. Crates bob gently in the void, some still sealed. Something skitters in the shadows between them.

rooms.name: Gaping Hull Breach
rooms.description: A massive hole torn through the outer hull reveals layers of internal structure. The edges are sharp and corroded. Useful salvage clings to the exposed framework.

rooms.name: Ruined Control Room
rooms.description: Banks of dead consoles line the walls, their screens dark and cracked. A corroded automaton stands frozen mid-step near the captain's chair, its optics flickering with residual power.

rooms.name: Engine Room Wreckage
rooms.description: The heart of the ship, now still. Massive turbines lie split open, their cores dark. Among the wreckage, a faintly glowing device pulses with residual energy — the Stabilization Engine.

enemies_data.name: Void Vermin Pack
enemies_data.description: A chittering swarm of small, multi-legged creatures with translucent shells. Individually weak, they overwhelm through numbers.

enemies_data.name: Void Vermin Scout
enemies_data.description: A lone vermin, larger than its pack cousins. Its mandibles click as it sizes you up.

enemies_data.name: Corroded Automaton
enemies_data.description: A maintenance robot gone wrong. Its corroded frame jerks with erratic, dangerous movements. Sparks fly from exposed wiring.

coral_thicket.name: The Coral Thicket
coral_thicket.description: A dense growth of crystallized organic matter, glowing with soft bioluminescence. The coral structures twist and branch in impossible patterns, creating a labyrinth of beauty and danger.

rooms.name: Coral Threshold
rooms.description: The edge of the thicket glows with pale blue light. Crystalline tendrils reach outward like fingers, and the air tastes faintly sweet. The path back to the skerry lies to the west.

rooms.name: Luminous Grove
rooms.description: A clearing in the coral where enormous fan-shaped growths create a canopy of shifting light. Someone has been living here — there's a crude lean-to built against a coral wall.

rooms.name: Narrow Coral Tunnel
rooms.description: A tight passage between coral walls, barely wide enough to squeeze through. The walls pulse with internal light, and thorny protrusions scrape against anything that passes.

rooms.name: Spore Chamber
rooms.description: A vast hollow space filled with drifting clouds of luminous spores. They pulse in slow rhythms, beautiful but potentially toxic. The air is thick and warm.

rooms.name: Heart of the Thicket
rooms.description: The deepest part of the coral growth, where the crystal structures are oldest and most complex. A woman tends a small garden of cultivated coral, humming to herself. Nestled among the roots, a lattice-work of living crystal pulses with green light.

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler
enemies_data.description: A coral-armored predator that blends with the thicket walls. It strikes from ambush, its barbed limbs lashing out with surprising speed.

enemies_data.name: Thorn Crawler Alpha
enemies_data.description: The largest of its kind, this crawler has grown thick coral plating and developed a cunning patience. It waits until prey is committed before striking.

enemies_data.name: Spore Cloud
enemies_data.description: Not a creature exactly — a living cloud of luminous spores that drifts with purpose. Inhaling them causes disorientation and hallucinations.

frozen_wreck.name: The Frozen Wreck
frozen_wreck.description: An ancient ship, petrified and encrusted with void-ice. It drifts in eerie silence, its surfaces glittering with frost. Whatever happened here happened long ago, and some things are best left undisturbed.

rooms.name: Ice-Crusted Bow
rooms.description: The prow of the ancient vessel juts from a mass of void-ice. The metal beneath is unlike anything you've seen — dark, dense, and covered in script that seems to shift when you're not looking directly at it. The way back is to the south.

rooms.name: Frost-Locked Corridor
rooms.description: A long hallway sheathed in ice so thick the walls are barely visible. Your breath freezes in the air. Shadows move beneath the ice — whether preserved crew or something else, you can't tell.

rooms.name: Observation Deck
rooms.description: A domed room with panels of translucent crystal, now frosted over. Through the ice you can see the void stretching in all directions. A figure sits at a desk covered in notes and rubbings of the ship's inscriptions.

rooms.name: Crew Quarters
rooms.description: Rows of alcoves line the walls, each containing a frozen shape that might once have been a sleeping berth — or a coffin. The ice here is thinnest, and you can almost make out faces.

rooms.name: Ancient Armory
rooms.description: Weapon racks line the walls, but the weapons have long since fused with the ice. Only one corner remains accessible, where a warrior has set up camp amid the frozen arsenal.

rooms.name: The Vault
rooms.description: The deepest chamber of the ancient ship. The ice here is clear as glass, and through it you can see the walls are covered in intricate murals depicting a civilization of tree-like beings. At the center, encased in a pillar of crystal, rests a small structure — perfect, ornate, unmistakably a house built for beings no larger than your hand.

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade
enemies_data.description: A shadow that moves beneath the ice, reaching through with freezing tendrils. It was something once — crew, passenger, prisoner. Now it's just cold and hunger.

enemies_data.name: Swarm Mites
enemies_data.description: Tiny crystalline insects that swarm in vast numbers. Each one is fragile, but together they can strip metal to the bone.

enemies_data.name: Frost Shade Elder
enemies_data.description: The captain of the frozen wreck, preserved in void-ice for centuries. Its form is massive, translucent, and filled with a terrible cold intelligence. It guards the vault and the Eliok House within.

df_entrance.name: Edge of the Debris Field
df_entrance.description: Jagged metal fragments drift lazily at the boundary of the debris field. A faint hum resonates through the larger pieces, as if the wreckage still remembers being whole. The way back to the skerry is clear to the south.

df_cargo_bay.name: Shattered Cargo Bay
df_cargo_bay.description: The remnants of a cargo hold, its walls peeled open like a tin can. Crates bob gently in the void, some still sealed. Something skitters in the shadows between them.

df_hull_breach.name: Gaping Hull Breach

df_control_room.name: Ruined Control Room

df_engine_room.name: Engine Room Wreckage

ct_spore_chamber.name: Spore Chamber

fw_quarters.name: Crew Quarters
fw_quarters.description: Rows of alcoves line the walls, each containing a frozen shape that might once have been a sleeping berth — or a coffin. The ice here is thinnest, and you can almost make out faces.

fw_armory.name: Ancient Armory
fw_armory.description: Weapon racks line the walls, but the weapons have long since fused with the ice. Only one corner remains accessible, where a warrior has set up camp amid the frozen arsenal.

fw_vault.name: The Vault
fw_vault.description: The deepest chamber of the ancient ship. The ice here is clear as glass, and through it you can see the walls are covered in intricate murals depicting a civilization of tree-like beings. At the center, encased in a pillar of crystal, rests a small structure — perfect, ornate, unmistakably a house built for beings no larger than your hand.

skerry_central.name: Central Clearing
skerry_central.description: The heart of the skerry — a small patch of solid ground amid the void. A faint warmth radiates from the world seed's presence below. From here, paths lead to the other areas of your fragile home.

skerry_shelter.name: Basic Shelter
skerry_shelter.description: A rough structure cobbled together from salvaged hull plating and fabric. It keeps the void-wind out, barely. There's room for a few people to sleep, though comfort is a generous word for it.

skerry_hollow.name: The Hollow
skerry_hollow.description: A natural depression in the skerry's surface where the world seed has taken root. Faint green light pulses from below, and you can feel its awareness pressing gently against your mind. This is where you commune with the seed and feed it motes.

skerry_landing.name: Landing Pad
skerry_landing.description: The edge of the skerry where the void begins. From here, you can see the distant glimmer of the debris field to the north, the soft glow of the coral thicket to the east, and the cold glitter of the frozen wreck to the west. This is where explorers depart and return.

metal_scraps.name: Metal Scraps
metal_scraps.description: Twisted pieces of hull plating and structural supports. Useful for building and crafting.

wire.name: Wire
wire.description: Lengths of copper and fiber-optic cabling salvaged from the wreckage.

torn_fabric.name: Torn Fabric
torn_fabric.description: Scraps of durable ship fabric. Worn but still strong enough to be useful.

basic_tools.name: Basic Tools
basic_tools.description: A collection of wrenches, pliers, and a multi-driver. The essentials.

luminous_moss.name: Luminous Moss
luminous_moss.description: Softly glowing moss from the coral thicket. It pulses with a gentle blue-green light.

resin.name: Resin
resin.description: Thick, amber-colored resin from coral growths. Hardens quickly and bonds well.

seeds.name: Seeds
seeds.description: Hardy seeds from coral-adapted plants. They could grow in Tuft-nourished soil.

coral_fragments.name: Coral Fragments
coral_fragments.description: Broken pieces of crystallized coral. Sharp-edged and surprisingly strong.

ancient_alloys.name: Ancient Alloys
ancient_alloys.description: Metal from the frozen wreck, made of alloys unknown to any living smith. Dense, dark, and warm to the touch.

frozen_water.name: Frozen Water
frozen_water.description: Ice from the frozen wreck. Pure and clean once thawed — precious in the void.

preserved_food.name: Preserved Food
preserved_food.description: Vacuum-sealed rations from the frozen wreck. Ancient but still edible.

crystal_shards.name: Crystal Shards
crystal_shards.description: Fragments of crystalline material that hum faintly. They react to Tuft's presence.

rope.name: Rope
rope.description: Strong braided cord made from fabric and resin. Essential for building.

torch.name: Torch
torch.description: A wire-wrapped bundle of luminous moss that casts a soft, steady glow.

shelter_patch.name: Shelter Patch
shelter_patch.description: A sturdy patch of fabric and coral for repairing structures.

water_filter.name: Water Filter
water_filter.description: A crystal and resin filtration device. Purifies void-mist into drinkable water.

signal_beacon.name: Signal Beacon
signal_beacon.description: A powerful light that can be seen far across the void. Might attract help — or trouble.

stabilization_engine.name: Stabilization Engine
stabilization_engine.description: A compact device that hums with residual power. It was designed to keep something steady — a ship, a platform, a world. In your hands, it steadies your own work.
stabilization_engine.discovery_text: Among the wreckage of the turbines, you spot a device that's still intact — still humming. The Stabilization Engine. It pulses faintly, as if waiting to be useful again.

growth_lattice.name: Growth Lattice
growth_lattice.description: A living framework of crystallized coral that pulses with green light. It resonates with organic growth, amplifying the connection between living things and the void.
growth_lattice.discovery_text: Nestled among the oldest coral roots, a lattice of living crystal pulses with deep green light. The Growth Lattice. Tuft stirs in your awareness — it can feel this thing, and it wants it.

eliok_house.name: Eliok House
eliok_house.description: A miniature house of impossible craftsmanship, built for beings no larger than your hand. Every surface is carved with scenes of a great tree sheltering a civilization. It radiates warmth and a sense of home so powerful it makes your eyes sting.
eliok_house.discovery_text:
The crystal pillar cracks as you touch it, and the tiny house falls gently into your hands. It's warm. Impossibly warm. Inside, you can see furniture, tapestries, a hearth with a fire that somehow still burns. This is the Eliok House — a home that remembers what home means.

Feed it to Tuft for 25 motes — the largest single offering possible.
Or keep it, and it will transform into proper NPC housing on the skerry, complete with a tiny dryad companion who tends the gardens.
eliok_house.keep_effect: The Eliok House expands when placed on the skerry, becoming a full-sized dwelling of living wood. A small dryad emerges — a remnant of the Eliok civilization — and begins tending the gardens. All NPC mood bonuses increase. All NPCs gain +1 loyalty. A new aspect appears on the skerry: 'Memory of the Eliok Lives On.'
eliok_house.feed_effect: Tuft absorbs the Eliok House and surges with growth. 25 motes flood into the world seed. For a moment, you see what Tuft sees — a vision of the great tree the Eliok once tended, stretching across an entire world. Then the vision fades, but Tuft remembers.

rope.name: Rope
rope.description: Braid fabric and coat with resin for a strong, flexible rope.

torch.name: Torch
torch.description: Wrap luminous moss around a wire frame for a steady, lasting light.

basic_tools.name: Basic Tools
basic_tools.description: Shape metal scraps into simple but effective hand tools.

shelter_patch.name: Shelter Patch
shelter_patch.description: Reinforce fabric with coral fragments for a durable repair patch.

water_filter.name: Water Filter
water_filter.description: Build a filtration device using crystal resonance to purify void-mist.

signal_beacon.name: Signal Beacon
signal_beacon.description: Craft a powerful beacon from ancient metals and bioluminescent moss.

explorer_events.name: Void Wind
explorer_events.description: A sudden gust of void-wind buffets you, threatening to knock you off course.
explorer_events.success: You brace yourself and weather the wind. It passes quickly.
explorer_events.failure: The wind catches you off guard. You lose your footing and take a hard tumble.

explorer_events.name: Hidden Cache
explorer_events.description: You spot something glinting in a crevice — a hidden cache of supplies.
explorer_events.success: You find a small cache of useful materials!
explorer_events.failure: You reach for it, but it's just a reflection. Nothing here.

explorer_events.name: Strange Sound
explorer_events.description: An eerie humming fills the air, making your teeth vibrate.
explorer_events.success: You focus your mind and the sound fades. Just the void playing tricks.
explorer_events.failure: The sound gets inside your head. Your concentration wavers.

explorer_events.name: Tuft's Warning
explorer_events.description: Tuft pulses urgently — it senses something nearby.
explorer_events.success: Tuft's awareness reveals a hidden danger in the next room. You'll be prepared.

homekeeper_events.name: NPC Argument
homekeeper_events.description: Two of your settlers are having a heated disagreement about how to organize supplies.
homekeeper_events.success: You mediate the argument successfully. Both parties feel heard.
homekeeper_events.failure: The argument escalates. Both NPCs are in worse moods.

homekeeper_events.name: Resource Shortage
homekeeper_events.description: Supplies are running low. The settlers are worried about food and water.
homekeeper_events.success: You redistribute supplies efficiently. Crisis averted.
homekeeper_events.failure: The shortage strains morale. Everyone tightens their belts.

homekeeper_events.name: Tuft Growth Spurt
homekeeper_events.description: Tuft pulses with sudden energy, its roots spreading visibly.
homekeeper_events.success: Tuft grows a little stronger! The skerry feels more alive.

homekeeper_events.name: Void Debris
homekeeper_events.description: A chunk of debris drifts close to the skerry.
homekeeper_events.success: You spot useful materials in the debris and salvage them!
homekeeper_events.failure: The debris drifts past before you can reach it.

homekeeper_events.name: Peaceful Day
homekeeper_events.description: The void is calm today. A rare moment of tranquility on the skerry.
homekeeper_events.success: Everyone rests well. The quiet is a gift.

homekeeper_events.name: NPC Request
homekeeper_events.description: One of the settlers approaches you with a personal request.
homekeeper_events.success: You take time to listen and help. Their loyalty increases.
homekeeper_events.failure: You're too busy to give them proper attention. They understand, but they're disappointed.

void_vermin_pack.name: Void Vermin Pack
void_vermin_pack.description: A chittering swarm of small, multi-legged creatures with translucent shells. Individually weak, they overwhelm through numbers.

void_vermin_scout.name: Void Vermin Scout
void_vermin_scout.description: A lone vermin, larger than its pack cousins. Its mandibles click as it sizes you up.

corroded_automaton.name: Corroded Automaton
corroded_automaton.description: A maintenance robot gone wrong. Its corroded frame jerks with erratic, dangerous movements. Sparks fly from exposed wiring.

thorn_crawler.name: Thorn Crawler
thorn_crawler.description: A coral-armored predator that blends with the thicket walls. It strikes from ambush, its barbed limbs lashing out with surprising speed.

thorn_crawler_alpha.name: Thorn Crawler Alpha
thorn_crawler_alpha.description: The largest of its kind, this crawler has grown thick coral plating and developed a cunning patience. It waits until prey is committed before striking.

spore_cloud.name: Spore Cloud
spore_cloud.description: Not a creature exactly — a living cloud of luminous spores that drifts with purpose. Inhaling them causes disorientation and hallucinations.

frost_shade.name: Frost Shade
frost_shade.description: A shadow that moves beneath the ice, reaching through with freezing tendrils. It was something once — crew, passenger, prisoner. Now it's just cold and hunger.

swarm_mites.name: Swarm Mites
swarm_mites.description: Tiny crystalline insects that swarm in vast numbers. Each one is fragile, but together they can strip metal to the bone.

frost_shade_elder.name: Frost Shade Elder
frost_shade_elder.description: The captain of the frozen wreck, preserved in void-ice for centuries. Its form is massive, translucent, and filled with a terrible cold intelligence. It guards the vault and the Eliok House within.

event_log: Day 1: You arrived at the skerry, a tiny island of solid ground in the endless void.
